Tips
● Strength
Monday
Upper Body
~50 min  ·  5 warm-up + 40 lifting + 5 cool-down
Exercises
01
Warm-up walk or light bike
5 min
02
Dumbbell chest press
or push-ups on knees to start
3 × 10–12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
03
Pec deck
full stretch at open, squeeze hard at close
3 × 12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
04
Seated cable row
or dumbbell bent-over row
3 × 10–12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
05
Dumbbell shoulder press
3 × 10–12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
06
Lat pulldown
or assisted pull-up machine
3 × 10–12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
07
Dumbbell bicep curl
2 × 12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
08
Tricep rope pushdown
2 × 12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
09
Stretching cool-down
5 min
Rest 60–90 sec between sets. Pick a weight where the last 2–3 reps are hard but form stays clean. Add 5 lbs when all reps feel easy.
● Rest Day
Tuesday
Active Recovery
Light movement only — let your muscles rebuild
Recovery Day
Aim for 7,000+ steps of light walking today.

A 10–15 min walk after meals lowers blood sugar spikes by up to 30%.
Soreness is normal. Severe pain or dark urine — call your doctor.
● Cardio
Wednesday
Zone 2 Cardio
~40 min  ·  Pick one option below — 30 min steady Zone 2
Choose your cardio
🚶 Treadmill
Speed3.5 mph
Incline4–5%
Duration30 min
NoteIncline bumps HR without faster pace
🚴 Stationary Bike
ResistanceLevel 8–10 / 20
Cadence70–80 RPM
Duration30 min
NoteAdjust resistance if HR drifts out of zone
🏃 Outdoor Walk
Pace3.5–4 mph
RouteHilly if possible
Duration30 min
NoteUse GPS app to track pace
🪜 Stairmaster
SpeedLevel 5–7
Duration30 min
NoteLight touch on rails only — don't lean
Talk test: Short sentence yes, full conversation no. Target heart rate: 100–130 bpm. Adjust settings up or down on your first session to land in that range.
● Strength
Thursday
Lower Body
~50 min  ·  Biggest blood sugar benefit of the week
Exercises
01
Warm-up walk
5 min
02
Barbell squat
bar on upper traps, chest up, drive through heels
4 × 8–10
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
03
Romanian deadlift
dumbbells, hinge at hips, slight knee bend
3 × 10
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
04
Leg extension machine
start light — stop if you feel sharp knee pain
3 × 12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
05
Leg curl machine
lying face down, curl heels toward glutes
3 × 12
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
06
Seated calf raises
3 × 15
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
07
Cool-down + hip flexor stretch
5 min
Barbell squat is your anchor lift. 4 sets instead of 3 — it's the most testosterone-boosting, glucose-burning movement in the plan. Take 2 min rest between squat sets.
● Rest Day
Friday
Active Recovery
Light movement only
Recovery Day
Legs will be sore from Thursday. Walk, stretch, or foam roll.

Aim for 7,000+ steps today.
● Cardio + Core
Saturday
Cardio + Core
~50 min  ·  Cardio + serious core work
Cardio first
01
Warm-up walk or easy bike
5 min
02
Zone 2 cardio — your choice
see Wednesday for settings
25 min
Core circuit
03
Russian twist
hold a weight plate — feet raised off floor
3 × 20 reps
Log weight used
lbs
✓ Saved!
04
Hanging knee raises
captain's chair or pull-up bar, slow and controlled
3 × 12–15
05
Ab wheel rollout
start on knees — roll out slow, pull back controlled
3 × 8–10
06
Cool-down stretch
5 min
Ab wheel rollout is brutally effective — start shallow if needed. Russian twists hit your obliques. Hanging raises hit lower abs. Together these three cover the entire core.
● Full Rest
Sunday
Full Rest
Recovery is where the gains actually happen
Rest & Recharge
Full rest today. Sleep, eat well, rebuild. Aim for 7–8 hours of sleep — poor sleep directly raises blood sugar.

A short evening walk is welcome but not required.
● Reference
Key Tips &
Guidelines
Statin + prediabetes specific rules
150+
min cardio per week
7%
body weight goal (~15 lbs)
4
gym days per week
Daily habits
🚶
Walk 10–15 min after mealsCuts post-meal blood sugar spikes by up to 30%
💧
Drink 64+ oz of water dailyEspecially important on atorvastatin
🌡️
Always warm up before lifting5 min minimum — cold muscles tear more easily
🧘
Never skip your cool-downPrevents dizziness and lowers injury risk
😴
7–8 hours sleep every nightPoor sleep raises blood sugar directly
📈
Add 5 lbs when all reps feel easyProgressive overload = continued progress
📅
Check progress every 4 weeksWeight, energy levels, and how clothes fit
Atorvastatin warning: Normal post-workout soreness is fine. Severe muscle pain, weakness, or dark/brown urine — stop and call your doctor immediately.
